tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: Through Tears to Glory: 21-Year-Old Linda Noskova Wins Her First Grand Slam
stories.summary: After wasting five match points, 21-year-old Linda Noskova saw the Venus Rosewater Dish in the bathroom and promised herself the big trophy, then dominated the third set to win her first Grand Slam at Wimbledon.

stories.headline: From Pantry Visitor to 20-Pantry Founder: Her Childhood Shame Became Her Life's Mission
stories.summary: A woman who once relied on food pantries now runs 20 judgment-free community pantries across Michigan, honoring the dignity of every family she serves.

stories.headline: Three Generations, One Mission: Family Helps Chester County Reach Functional Zero Homelessness
stories.summary: Minnie McNeil, 85, and her family's four decades of devoted work alongside her son Andrew, 58, and grandson Derrick, 27, helped Chester County reach functional zero for chronic homelessness.

stories.headline: One Meal at a Time: Dubai Engineer Brightens Delivery Riders' Lives With Surprise Dinners
stories.summary: Turkish software engineer Tarik Toptas, 24, has donated more than 240 meals to Dubai's Careem delivery riders in two weeks, watching their faces light up with gratitude.

stories.headline: Numbats Rise From the Brink: 30 Years of Dedication Pays Off
stories.summary: From a low of just 300 in the late 1970s, Western Australia's numbats now number between 2,000 and 3,000 after three decades of collaborative conservation efforts removed them from the endangered species list.

stories.headline: Wedding Cake Rescue: An 81-Year-Old Baker Steps In With Stunning Creations
stories.summary: When a couple's wedding cake fell apart Friday morning, 81-year-old baker Elria Billson stepped in with no notice and guided the family through the night, delivering stunning cakes by 21:00.

stories.headline: Like Fine Wine: 50-Year-Old Ludo Pommeret Wins Third Consecutive Hardrock 100
stories.summary: French trail runner Ludo Pommeret, 50, won his third consecutive Hardrock 100 ultramarathon, breaking his own record by 20 minutes through Colorado's 101.8-mile mountain gauntlet.

stories.headline: A Generous Cut: Young Boy Donates a Foot of Hair to Help Children Regain Confidence
stories.summary: Eleven-year-old Jackson McGrath-Merton grew his hair for a year and a half, then donated 12 inches to Wigs for Kids to help children regain confidence after hair loss.

stories.headline: Neeru Dhanda Makes History: First Indian Woman Trap Shooter to Win ISSF World Cup Gold
stories.summary: Indian shooter Naib Subedar Neeru Dhanda made history as the first Indian woman trap shooter to win ISSF World Cup gold, breaking the national record with a 121/125 qualification score.

stories.headline: From Trafficking to Freedom: Five Spider Monkeys Find New Life in Mexican Biosphere
stories.summary: Five spider monkeys rescued from illegal trafficking—three females and two males—bonded as a stable family unit and were released into Mexico's La Encrucijada Biosphere Reserve.

stories.headline: A First in Space: Human Kidney and Liver Tissue Bioprinted Aboard the ISS
stories.summary: Aboard the International Space Station in June, scientists successfully bioprinted human kidney and liver tissue plus 28 nerve repair implants for the first time, a breakthrough for manufacturing replacement organs.

stories.headline: Freedom Restored: 37 Western Pond Turtles Released After 35 Years of Care
stories.summary: After 35 years of species recovery work, Washington's Woodland Park Zoo and wildlife teams released 37 young western pond turtles, part of a program that has head-started more than 2,300 turtles.

stories.headline: Blood to Sperm: Scientists Create Early-Stage Reproductive Cells in Lab Breakthrough
stories.summary: Researchers transform human blood cells into early-stage sperm, marking major progress toward lab-grown solutions for infertility.
